bce.event: Appearance of Homo Sapiens Sapiens

Earliest modern human arrived in India from the south because of an early coastal migration from Africa, however the Bori artefacts found in Pune, Maharashtra pushes the date further back to 14 lakh BCE

Late or Upper Palaeolithic Age; Paintings in caves/rock shelters (27,500); Cave paintings at Bhimbetka (Madhya Pradesh); Homo sapiens fossils (25,500, Sri Lanka);

Earliest sign of cultivation in India; Domestication of cattle and dogs in Africa

Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in Amritsar, Punjab
End of World War I; Treaty of Versailles signed between Germany and Allied Powers [ USA, Russia, UK, France, Italy, Japan ]
First issue of Navjeeven (Gujarati Newspaper) with MK Gandhi as editor was published
First issue of Young India (English Newspaper) with MK Gandhi as editor was published
Government of India Act 1919 passed by UK Parliament
Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was transformed to Aligarh Muslim University
The Khilafat Committee launched Non-Cooperation Movement
Foundation All India Trade Union Congress
Satyendra Prasanna Sinha becomes the first Governor of Bihar & Odisha Presidency
Moplah or Mapillah or Malabar Rebellion; Muslim peasants of Kerala rebelled against British and Hindu Landlords
Three presidency banks merged to create Imperial Bank of India
Sarabandi (No-Tax) campaign led by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
Southern Ireland seceded from United Kingdom to form United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Island
Chauri Chaura incident; Gandhi called off Non-Cooperation Movement
Indian National Congress - Gaya Session, chaired by Chittaranjan Das; Showcased ideological differences between Gandhian and Swarajists
Foundation of Swaraj Party by Chittaranjan Das and Motilal Nehru
Swaraj Dal in Bihar was established by Krishna Sinha
Hindustan Republican Association (HRA) was formed by revolutionary nationalists to organise armed revolution

Vaikom Satyagraha in present day Kerala; People belonging to low-caste were not allowed to walk on the roads leading to Vaikom Mahadev Temple; They led a peaceful non-violent protest against this castiest ban.
Mahatma Gandhi presided Belgaum INC session; Only INC session presided by Gandhi
Kakori Conspiracy - Train carrying revenue money were looted by HRA members
Maharaja Hari Singh ascends to the throne of Jammu & Kashmir
Foundation of RSS - Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh by Dr. Keshav Baliram Hedgewar
The Butler Committee, officially known as the Indian States Committee, was appointed to review and define the relationship between the Indian princely states and the British Crown.
All India State People Conference was set up by Princely Sates
Foundation of HSRA - Hindustan Socialist Republican Association; Chandra Shekhar Azad remaned HRA
Vallabhbhai Patel led Bardoli Satyagraha against unjustified tax hike; Earned the title - Sardar
Raman Effect discovered by CV Raman
Motilal Nehru presented Nehru Report as a response to Simon Commission; It was the first Indian effort to draft a constitution for India, led by Indians themselves
Lala Lajpat Rai led a protest against Simon Commission giving slogan - Simon Go Back; He got fatally injured due to police lathicharge ordered by Superintendent James Scott
Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev and Rajguru killed John P. Saunders instead of James Scott
Swami Sahajanand Saraswati founder Bihar Provincial Kisan Shabha (BPKS) to mobilise peasants agains zamindari system in Bihar
Child Marriage restraint act was passed
The Great Depression
Kameshwar Singh became the last ruling Maharaja of Darbhanga
Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw a bomb in the central legislative assembly

Lahore Session of Congress; Jawaharlal Nehru was made president of INC, replacing his father Motilal Nehru as president; Poorna Swaraj was declared as objective of Congress; Jawaharlal Nehru hoisted Tri-Colour Flag of India's Independence; 26 January 1930 was to be clebrated as Poorna Swaraj Day
Forest Satyagraha
First Round Table Conference in London to discuss Simon Commission Report
Purna Swaraj Day; Independence Day was observed all over India
MK Gandhi started Civil Disobedience Movement with Dandi March {From Sabarmati to Dandi} to break Salt Law
MK Gandhi reached Dandi, covering 241 miles in 24 days
The Indian Nation - A nationalist newspaper began its publication in Patna, Bihar launched by Maharaja Kameshwar Singh of Darbhanga
Economist Dr. V. K. R. V. Rao made first scientific and systematic attempt to estimate India’s national income
The Gandhi-Irwin Pact (also called the Delhi Pact) was a political agreement signed between Mahatma Gandhi and Irwin, the Viceroy of India.
Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev and Rajguru were executed.
Karachi Session of INC presided by Vallabhbhai Patel; Adoption of Karachi Resolution on Fundamental Rights and Economic Policies
Dogra soldiers of Maharaja Hari Singh of Kashmir shot 22 muslim protestors; Observed as Kashmir Martyrs' Day
Gandhi went to Englad to attend Second Round Table Conference in London, according to Gandhi-Irwin Pact
Third Round Table Conference was held in England that would later influence Government of India Act 1935

The Communal Award was announced by British Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald to grant separate electorates to various religious and social communities in British India, including Dalits (Depressed Classes)
The All India Harijan Sevak Sangh was founded by Mahatma Gandhi to work for the upliftment of the Dalits (then called Harijans) and to eradicate untouchability
Poona Pact; Between Ambedkar and Gandhi; Separate electorates for Dalits were replaced with reserved seats in joint electorates

Rahmat Ali proposed a separate homeland for the Muslims of north-western India in his pamphlet - Now or Never: Are We to Live or Perish Forever?; First public use of the term "Pakstan" (without the "i")
Civil Disobedience Movement was called off by Congress
Formation of Congress Socialist Party by Acharya Narendra Dev, Jayaprakash Naryan, Ram Manohar Lohia and others
Reserve Bank of India Act was enacted to constitute RBI; First RBI Governor - Sir Osborne Smith
Government of India Act 1935 passed by UK Parliament
Odisha (then Orissa) carved out of Bihar and Orissa based on language (Odia-speaking areas).
All India Kisan Shabha (AIKS) was established with Sahajanand Sarasawati as it's first president
Indian Provincial Election was conducted
Discovery of Nuclear Fission
INC Haripura (Gujarat Session); Subhash Chandra Bose elected as president for the first time
INC Tripuri (Madhya Pradesh) Session; Subhash re-elected as president of congress against Pattabhi Sitaramaiya, supported by Gandhi
Rajkot Satyagraha in Gujarat; Mahatma Gandhi kept fast-unto-death against autocratic rule of Rajkot ruler
Subhash Chandra Bose founded Forward Bloc within Congress after his resignation as President
World War II; Allies [UK, Soviet Union, USA, France] vs Axis [Germany, Italy, Japan]
Sardar Uddham Singh assassinated Micheal O'Dwyer as a retaliation against Jallianwala Bagh Massacre

The August Proposal - commonly referred to as the August Offer—was made by the British Viceroy of India, Linlithgow, during World War II. Its main aim was to secure Indian cooperation for the British war effort by promising certain constitutional reforms after the end of the war.
Subhash Chandra Bose left culcutta went Singapore via Germany
The Cripps Proposal put by Sir Stafford Cripps. The goal was to secure Indian support for the British war effort by making post war political concessions.
Indian Independence League officially formed at the Bangkok Conference by Rash Behari Bose
Quit India Movement; Formation of Indian National Army; Revolt of 1942; MK Gandhi gave his historic call - Do or Die
Viceroy Lilinthgow ordered to fire on the unarmed protestors in and around Patna
C. D. Deshmukh became first Indian to be RBI Governor
Archibald Wavell became the Governor-General {Viceroy} of India
Subhash Chandra Bose declared formation of Azad Hind Fauz in Singapore
Bose's historic visit to Port Blair as the Head of the Provisional Government of Azad Hind; Subhas Chandra Bose renamed the Andman Islands as "Shaheed Dweep" and the Nicobar Islands as "Swaraj Dweep"
Bretton Woods Agreement (1944) was an international monetary agreement aimed at creating a stable post-World War II economic order
Invention of ENIAC- the first electronic, General-Purpose Computer
International Monetary Fund (IMF) established based on Bretton Woods Agreement.
End of WWII in Europe; Victory in Europe Day
Formation of United Nations
Clement Attlee of Labour Party became Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
USA dropped an uranium-based atomic bomb (Little Boy) on Hiroshima, Japan
USA dropped a plutonium-based atomic bomb (Fat Man) on Nagasaki, Japan
End of WWII in Japan; Victory in Japan Day
End of World War II
Royal Indian Navy Revolt; Sailors at HMIS Talwar in Bombay strike against British officers
Cabinet Mission arrived Delhi; British Cabinet led by Clement Attlee sent three member mission to delhi to negotiate terms of power transfer
Election of Constituent Assembly of India (undivided) completed
Direct Action Day called by All India Muslim League led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah to demand creation of Pakistan
Interim Government of India formed under Cabinet Mission plan until new Constitution is ready
The first session of Constituent Assembly of India temporarily presided by Sachchidanand Sinha
Rajendra Prasad elected as permanent Chairman of Constituent Assembly
Lord Mountbatten becomes the Governor-General {Viceroy} of India
William Francis Hare becomes the last Secretary of State for India
Indian Independence Act 1947 passed
India became Independent
First war between India & Pakistan - War of Kashmir
Employee State Insuarance Act passed to provide social security to employees in case of sickness, maternity, disability, or death due to employment injury
Assassination of MK Gandhi by Nathuram Godse
India annexed Junagadh province
Ceylon became an independent dominion under the British Commonwealth; D.S. Senanayake became the first Prime Minister.
India invaded state of Hyderabad - Operation Polo
THE RESERVE BANK (TRANSFER TO PUBLIC OWNERSHIP) ACT, I948
RBI was nationalised.
Banking Regulation Act was enacted to increase power of RBI
Mao Zedong, leader of Communist Party of China established People's Republic of China
Constitutent Assembly ratified Constitution of India
Election Commission of India established
Indian Constitution came into effect
Government set up Planning Commission
Battle of Chamdo; PLA (People’s Liberation Army) invades eastern Tibet (Chamdo)
Mother Teresa founded the Missionaries of Charity in Kolkata
First Five Year Plan launched
First General Election concluded
Potti Sriramulu died of fasting - fighting for separate Andhra State
A new state Andhra was formed
Two civilian awards - Bharat Ratna and Padma Vibhushan were created
Panchsheel Agreement; Between India and China to guide peaceful coexistence - based on five principles (Panchsheel) of mutual respect and non-aggression.
Dadra and Nagar Haveli integrated in India
Leaders of Asian and African countries met at Bandung, Indonesia to discuss how they could continue to oppsose colonialism
Imperial Bank nationalised to create SBI
The state reorganisation commission submitted its report
Second Five Year Plan
Second Industrial Policy
The Life Insurance Corporation of India was established, when the Parliament of India passed the Life Insurance of India Act, nationalizing the insurance industry in India.
Balwantrai Mehta Committee; Recommended 3-tier Panchayati Raj system.
Bhilai Steel Plant was set up with the help of soviet union
14th Dalai Lama flees Tibet, crosses into India on 31 March and is given asylum. Establishes Tibetan government-in-exile in Dharamshala, Himachal Pradesh.
Three-tier Panchayati Raj first implemented in Nagaur, Rajasthan
Yuri Gagrin of USSR became the first person in the world to travel in space
India invaded Goa - Operation Vijay - Liberation of Goa
Indo-China War; China overpowered India
Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) was set up
In late 1960s GOI introduced HYV seeds to Indian farmers - Onset Green Revolution in India
Second India-Pakistan War
Border Security Force (BSF) established for a specialized border-guarding force
Research and Analysis Wing (RAW) was established to safeguard national interests through foreign intelligence, strategic monitoring, and covert operations.
The Government of India issued the Banking Companies (Acquisition and Transfer of Undertakings) Ordinance and 14 major banks operating in India were nationalised. Eg - PNB, BOB etc
Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) was established by Government of India
Bangladesh Liberation War
GOI launched Project Tiger
Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala; Established Basic Structure Doctrine – Parliament can amend Constitution but not its basic structure
India conducted it's first successful nuclear weapon test in Pokhran - Smiling Buddha
Jayaprakash Narayan called for a Total Revolution (Sampoorna Kranti) against Indira Gandhi Government
Sikkim became a full-fledged state of the Union of India
President Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed, on the advice of PM Indira Gandhi, declares a State of Emergency under Article 352 due to "internal disturbances"
42nd Amendment enacted; Added the words “Socialist,” “Secular,” and “Integrity” to the Preamble; Added Fundamental Duties;
Emergency officially lifted by Indira Gandhi; Lok Sabha dissolved; general elections announced.
Morarji Desai of Janta Party becomes the first non-Congress Prime Minister of India.
Mandal Commission appointed to identify the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBCs) in India and recommend steps for their advancement
First Judges Case; S.P. Gupta vs President Of India; Gave primacy to Executive in judicial appointments, i.e. the opinion of the Chief Justice of India was to be considered but was not binding
Sarkaria Commission; To examine and review the working of existing arrangements between the Union and the States, and recommend changes for better cooperative federalism.
Rakesh Sharma became the first Indian to travel to space
Operation Blue Star
P. V. Narasimha Rao was sworn in as the Prime Minister of India
Mandal Commission Case; Indra Sawhney vs Union of India; Upheld 50% cap on total reservation, 27% OBC reservation, introduced creamy layer concept.
National Panchayati Raj Day; 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act 1992 for local self government (panchayati raj) became effective
Second Judges Case; Established Collegium System – primacy to CJI in judicial appointments.
National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) established
World Trade Organisation (WTO) established
Third Judges Case; Expanded Collegium to CJI + 4 senior-most judges.
International Space Station was launched
Third India-Pakistan War - Kargil War - Operation Vijay
A new state Jharkhand was carved out of Bihar on the day Birsa Munda was born
Ministry of Panchayati Raj was created
The Right to Information Act passed
In Bihar JEEViKA was launched in 2006 under the Bihar Rural Livelihoods Promotion Society (BRLPS)
Bihar became the first state to take the lead by implementing 50% reservation for women (both in PRIs and urban local bodies)
Nepal became federal democratic republic
Right to Education included in Fundamental Rights
Niti Ayog was setup by GOI, replacing Planning Commission
GOI announced demonetisation of 500 and 1000 notes. New 500 and 2000 notes were issued.
GOI introduced Goods and Services Tax (GST)
First case of Coronavirus disease registered in Wuhan, China that caused pandemic worldwide
Bhartiya Nyay Samhita (BNS) came into effect repealing Indian Penal Code (IPC)
State of Punjab v. Davinder Singh; The Court held that states are constitutionally allowed to sub-classify SC and ST categories for welfare/reservations under Articles 15(4) and 16(4)
